mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 PAYEMS at 137,318k showing continued post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth periods produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable or declining, the real interest rate becomes deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and bonds. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, predicting positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 FEDFUNDS at 1.81% and CPIAUCSL at 218.88 both consistent with trigger
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 2.0% and inflation stable, 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 900,000 annualized units signal strong residential investment demand and consumer confidence, indicating late-cycle expansion. This sustained demand pressure supports equity returns through multiplier effects on construction employment and durable goods consumption over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST > 900k periods produce negative 60-day returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 6.0%, signaling slack in the economy and reduced pricing power. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns only when both conditions hold simultaneously, indicating demand weakness relative to supply capacity.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2014 INDPRO at 110.30 and March 2014 UNRATE at 6.1% both present
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with persistent slack and weak industrial momentum
failure_condition: When both INDPRO growth < 0.2% and UNRATE > 6.0%, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) accelerate month-over-month above 0.4% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive, indicating consumer demand is outpacing production capacity. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns as markets price in demand-driven earnings surprises and potential inflation expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2014 RSAFS at 375,473M showing sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: RSAFS growth > 0.4% produces negative 5-day returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window while payroll growth (PAYEMS) decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month, signaling labor market deterioration. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as markets reprice recession risk and earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k
evidence_refs: fact:March 2014 UNRATE at 6.1% and PAYEMS at 137,318k
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp and PAYEMS growth < 100k, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The spread between inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) widens to more than 1.5 percentage points, indicating real rates are becoming restrictive relative to inflation. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 60-day returns in late-cycle expansions where monetary tightening begins to constrain growth.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 FEDFUNDS at 1.81% and CPIAUCSL at 218.88 showing wide real rate spread
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with monetary tightening cycle beginning
failure_condition: When real rate spread > 1.5pp, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while capacity utilization remains below 80%, signaling that supply-side expansion is outpacing demand and productivity gains are being realized. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns as markets reward improving operational efficiency and margin expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2014 INDPRO at 110.30 showing post-crisis industrial recovery
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 0.3% produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
